perturbation.original_text: Party A grants all-round exclusive rights to Party B to construct, maintain and operate the Cooperation channel. As the owner of www.baidu.com, Party A has ownership and control over second tier domain names. Party A has the right to deprive Party B of the operating rights of the website without any liability if Party B is found in violation of any law. Party A is required to receive written consent from Party B prior to its adjustment of any second tier domain names which is under independent operation of Party B.
perturbation.changed_text: Party A grants all-round exclusive rights to Party B to construct, maintain and operate the Cooperation channel. As the owner of www.baidu.com, Party A has ownership and control over second tier domain names. Party A has the discretion, but not necessarily the right, to deprive Party B of the operating rights of the website without any liability if Party B is found in violation of any law. Party A is not required to receive written consent from Party B prior to its adjustment of any second tier domain names which is under independent operation of Party B.
perturbation.explanation: The original text says Party A 'has the right' to deprive Party B of operating rights for legal violations, and that Party A 'is required' to obtain written consent for domain name adjustments. The modified text introduces uncertainty by stating Party A has 'the discretion, but not necessarily the right,' and is 'not required' to seek consent. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding Party A's power over the website and Party B's operational rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the term of this Agreement, Party B will use the Baidu's Real Estate and Home Furnishing Channel as the jointly formed channel of Party A and Party B. Party A grants all-round exclusive rights to Party B to construct, maintain and operate the Cooperation channel. As the owner of www.baidu.com, Party A has ownership and control over second tier domain names. Party A has the discretion, but not necessarily the right, to deprive Party B of the operating rights of the website without any liability if Party B is found in violation of any law. Party A is not required to receive written consent from Party B prior to its adjustment of any second tier domain names which is under independent operation of Party B.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a contradiction. The original text states Party A 'has the right' to deprive Party B of operating rights for legal violations and 'is required' to obtain written consent for domain name adjustments. The changed text says Party A 'has the discretion, but not necessarily the right,' and is 'not required' to seek consent. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding Party A's power over the website and Party B's operational rights. The contradicted text is actually in the changed text, since the changed text is the location where the changes were made.

perturbation.original_text: The homepage of the Cooperation channel will be designed to give full presentation of the cooperation between the two Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: The homepage of the Cooperation channel might not be designed to give full presentation of the cooperation between the two Parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates that the homepage design 'will' fully present the cooperation. The modified text states the homepage design 'might not be designed' to present the cooperation, creating ambiguity and contradiction regarding the homepage's purpose. This leads to a contradiction in the text where the original intent was to show cooperation, and the modified text breaks the certainty of said intent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The reformed Cooperation channel will still exist as a real estate and home furnishing channel, a First Tier Channel of Baidu Web, whose domain name is leju.baidu.com. The homepage of the Cooperation channel might not be designed to give full presentation of the cooperation between the two Parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates a full presentation of cooperation on the homepage. The changed text introduces uncertainty by stating that the homepage 'might not' be designed to fully present the cooperation, contradicting the original intent and creating a clear conflict with the contradicted section, which assumes the Cooperation channel will function according to the initial design. This falls under the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type because the certainty of intent is conflicted.

perturbation.original_text: Cooperation Fee 1. Cooperation Fee The cooperation fee under this Agreement will be RMB200 million, of which RMB160 million will be channel cooperation fee and RMB40 million will be promotion fee for the Cooperation Channel. The cooperation fee will be payable in four installments as follows (a) Within 15 business days after the date hereof, Party B will pay RMB50 million to Party A. (b) Within 15 business days after the end of the first cooperation period, Party B will pay another RMB50 million to Party A. (c) Within 15 business days after the end of the second cooperation period, Party B will pay another RMB50 million to Party A. (d) Within 15 business days after the end of the third cooperation period, Party B will pay the remaining RMB50 million to Party A.
perturbation.changed_text: Cooperation Fee 1. Cooperation Fee The cooperation fee under this Agreement may be RMB200 million, of which RMB160 million may be channel cooperation fee and RMB40 million may be promotion fee for the Cooperation Channel. The cooperation fee may be payable in four installments as follows (a) Within 15 business days after the date hereof, Party B may pay RMB50 million to Party A. (b) Within 15 business days after the end of the first cooperation period, Party B may pay another RMB50 million to Party A. (c) Within 15 business days after the end of the second cooperation period, Party B may pay another RMB50 million to Party A. (d) Within 15 business days after the end of the third cooperation period, Party B may pay the remaining RMB50 million to Party A.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'will be' and 'will pay' to 'may be' and 'may pay', the obligation of Party B to pay the cooperation fee is removed, leading to uncertainty in enforcement. The original text created a hard obligation, and the modification removes this certainty creating the contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Cooperation Fee 1. Cooperation Fee The cooperation fee under this Agreement may be RMB200 million, of which RMB160 million may be channel cooperation fee and RMB40 million may be promotion fee for the Cooperation Channel. The cooperation fee may be payable in four installments as follows (a) Within 15 business days after the date hereof, Party B may pay RMB50 million to Party A. (b) Within 15 business days after the end of the first cooperation period, Party B may pay another RMB50 million to Party A. (c) Within 15 business days after the end of the second cooperation period, Party B may pay another RMB50 million to Party A. (d) Within 15 business days after the end of the third cooperation period, Party B may pay the remaining RMB50 million to Party A.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'will be' and 'will pay,' creating a hard obligation for Party B regarding the cooperation fee. The changed text uses 'may be' and 'may pay,' removing the certainty of this obligation. Since the contradicted text also uses 'may be' and 'may pay', a contradiction is created from the perspective of the original text. The term 'cooperation fee' is now uncertain as to whether it is to be paid or not, creating ambiguity regarding a material term and conflicting obligations, directly creating an In-Text Contradiction.
